33.reason: This condition incorrectly skips combining terms with exponent 0, and the loop only checks adjacent pairs once, missing cases where more than two terms have the same exponent.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  This condition incorrectly skips combining terms with exponent 0, and the loop only checks adjacent pairs once, missing cases where more than two terms have the same exponent.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: This loop only merges adjacent equal exponents, but after sorting, there may be more than two consecutive equal exponents. Also, the condition `c[i].pow!=0` incorrectly skips merging for exponent 0 terms. The correct approach is to use a nested loop to merge all equal exponents, as in the reference program.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Condition should not exclude exponent 0 and should use a nested loop to combine all occurrences of the same exponent, not just adjacent ones.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The condition c[i].pow!=0 incorrectly excludes exponent 0 terms from being combined. Additionally, only adjacent pairs are combined, leaving multiple same-exponent terms uncombined.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop goes up to n3-1, so accessing c[i+1] when i=n3-1 is out of bounds. Also, the condition c[i].pow!=0 is incorrect; we should merge terms regardless of whether the exponent is 0. The logic fails to correctly accumulate all terms with the same exponent because it only looks at the immediate next element and doesn't handle chains of same-exponent terms robustly if they are not adjacent after the first pass or if the zeroing out disrupts subsequent checks. More critically, accessing c[n3] is undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition c[i].pow!=0 is incorrect; terms with exponent 0 (constant terms) should also be merged if they have the same exponent. Additionally, accessing c[i+1] when i is n3-1 leads to out-of-bounds access, although logically the loop should handle merging all adjacent duplicates correctly without this specific exponent check. The primary logical error here is excluding exponent 0 from merging.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  // The condition `c[i].pow!=0` is incorrect; it skips merging terms with exponent 0. Also, this loop only merges adjacent duplicates once and doesn't handle multiple same exponents beyond two.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  This condition incorrectly skips combining terms when exponent is 0, and only checks adjacent pairs once, failing to combine all like terms.

32.reason: Loop bound should be i < n3-1 to avoid accessing c[n3]. Also the algorithm only checks adjacent pairs, failing to merge all terms with the same exponent.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Loop bound should be i < n3-1 to avoid accessing c[n3]. Also the algorithm only checks adjacent pairs, failing to merge all terms with the same exponent.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop bound should be i<n3-1 to avoid accessing c[i+1] out of bounds, and the combining logic must handle multiple terms with the same exponent.

39.reason: // Loop runs up to `n3-1`, but after merging, some zero-coefficient terms may remain unremoved if they are not adjacent or if merging was incomplete.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  Loop runs only up to n3-1, but after combining terms, some zero-coefficient terms may remain unremoved if they are not adjacent or if multiple terms share the same exponent beyond two.
